part_title: Section Two: Nursery and Transplant Management and Prevention of Pest Transfer to the Field
part_short_title: Nursery and Transplant Management and Prevention of Pest Transfer to the Field

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

The nursery is one of the most sensitive stages in an integrated management program for Tuta absoluta, because infested transplants may carry eggs or larvae into the field from the very beginning of the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery is one of the most sensitive stages in an integrated management program for Tuta absoluta, because infested transplants may carry eggs or larvae into the field from the very beginning of the season.

items.text: Infestation introduced with transplants differs from infestation caused by moth immigration from outside the field. When the pest is transferred with transplants, infestation may appear distributed throughout the field during the first few days, and larvae may begin establishing a new generation before pheromone traps record a clear increase in moth numbers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation introduced with transplants differs from infestation caused by moth immigration from outside the field. When the pest is transferred with transplants, infestation may appear distributed throughout the field during the first few days, and larvae may begin establishing a new generation before [[term:pheromone-trap]]pheromone traps[[/term]] record a clear increase in moth numbers.

items.text: Early infestation is particularly serious for the following reasons:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Early infestation is particularly serious for the following reasons:

items: The long period available for the pest to produce successive generations.
The small size of transplants and their limited ability to compensate for growing-point damage.
Difficulty of detecting eggs and recent mines.
Possibility of distributing infested transplants over a large area.
Movement of the pest from one nursery to several fields at the same time.
Higher cost of discarding plants after transplanting than removing them in the nursery.
Possibility of confusing Tuta infestation with transport injury, water stress, or nursery diseases.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The long period available for the pest to produce successive generations.
[[prepared]]The small size of transplants and their limited ability to compensate for growing-point damage.
[[prepared]]Possibility of distributing infested transplants over a large area.
[[prepared]]Movement of the pest from one nursery to several fields at the same time.
[[prepared]]Higher cost of discarding plants after transplanting than removing them in the nursery.
[[prepared]]Possibility of confusing Tuta infestation with transport injury, water stress, or nursery diseases.

items.text: Plants intended for planting, including tomato transplants and solanaceous hosts, are recognized pathways for the movement of Phthorimaea absoluta among sites and regions. Transplant health must therefore be treated as a basic requirement for initiating the integrated management program, not as a supplementary measure. [1, 2, 3]
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Plants intended for planting, including tomato transplants and [[term:solanaceae]]solanaceous hosts[[/term]], are recognized pathways for the movement of [[term:phthorimaea]]Phthorimaea absoluta[[/term]] among sites and regions. Transplant health must therefore be treated as a basic requirement for initiating the integrated management program, not as a supplementary measure. [1, 2, 3]

items.text: Objectives of Nursery Management
Nursery management aims to achieve the following:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nursery management aims to achieve the following:

items: 1. Prevent moths from entering the transplant-production area.
3. Detect infestation immediately after it appears.
7. Prevent pest movement with trays, containers, and transport vehicles.
8. Produce vigorous transplants capable of tolerating transplanting.
9. Maintain records allowing each batch to be traced.
10. Confirm that transplants are free from infestation before they leave the nursery.
11. Reinspect transplants upon arrival at the field.
12. Prevent surplus transplants from becoming a permanent pest reservoir.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Prevent moths from entering the transplant-production area.
[[prepared]]3. Detect infestation immediately after it appears.
[[prepared]]7. Prevent pest movement with trays, containers, and transport vehicles.
[[prepared]]8. Produce vigorous transplants capable of tolerating transplanting.
[[prepared]]9. Maintain records allowing each batch to be traced.
[[prepared]]10. Confirm that transplants are free from infestation before they leave the nursery.
[[prepared]]11. Reinspect transplants upon arrival at the field.
[[prepared]]12. Prevent surplus transplants from becoming a permanent pest reservoir.

items.text: These objectives cannot be achieved through preventive spraying alone; they require appropriate nursery design, sanitation, inspection, and batch traceability.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These objectives cannot be achieved through preventive spraying alone; they require appropriate nursery design, sanitation, inspection, and batch traceability.

items.text: The Nursery as a Critical Point in the Pest Life Cycle
Transplants possess several characteristics that make them suitable for oviposition, including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Transplants possess several characteristics that make them suitable for oviposition, including:

items: Presence of young and tender leaves.
High plant density within a limited area.
Ease of larval movement between contacting leaves.
Sufficient transplant-retention time for completion of part of the pest life cycle.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]High plant density within a limited area.
[[prepared]]Ease of larval movement between contacting leaves.
[[prepared]]Sufficient transplant-retention time for completion of part of the pest life cycle.

items.text: A single fertilized female entering the nursery may lay a large number of eggs on a group of transplants, particularly when traps and regular inspections are absent.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A single fertilized female entering the nursery may lay a large number of eggs on a group of transplants, particularly when traps and regular inspections are absent.

items.text: The effects of infestation are more severe in small transplants because a single mine or growing-point infestation may cause:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The effects of infestation are more severe in small transplants because a single mine or growing-point infestation may cause:

items.text: Selection of Nursery Location
The nursery should preferably be established at a site:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery should preferably be established at a site:

items: Away from old and infested tomato fields.
Away from tomato grading and packing areas.
Away from piles of rejected fruits.
Away from infested potato and eggplant fields.
Free from black nightshade and infested solanaceous hosts.
Not directly downwind from an infested field.
Where entry of workers and containers can be controlled.
Whose surroundings can be monitored regularly.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Away from old and infested tomato fields.
[[prepared]]Away from tomato grading and packing areas.
[[prepared]]Away from infested potato and eggplant fields.
[[prepared]]Free from black nightshade and infested solanaceous hosts.
[[prepared]]Not directly downwind from an infested field.
[[prepared]]Where entry of workers and containers can be controlled.

items.text: No single distance guarantees protection because moth movement depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single distance guarantees protection because moth movement depends on:

items.text: Assessment of the surrounding agricultural environment is therefore more important than relying on distance alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Assessment of the surrounding agricultural environment is therefore more important than relying on distance alone.

items.text: Buffer Zone Around the Nursery
A clean buffer zone should be established around the nursery and inspected regularly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A clean buffer zone should be established around the nursery and inspected regularly.

items.text: Management of the buffer zone includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Management of the buffer zone includes:

items: Removal of wild solanaceous species carrying active infestation.
Prevention of accumulation of tomato leaves and fruits.
Avoidance of storage of contaminated harvest crates.
Prevention of old or abandoned transplants remaining in the area.
Inspection of weeds before removal to determine whether the pest is present.
Installation of traps in locations suitable for detecting incoming moths.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Removal of wild solanaceous species carrying [[term:infestation-status]]active infestation[[/term]].
[[prepared]]Prevention of accumulation of tomato leaves and fruits.
[[prepared]]Avoidance of storage of contaminated harvest crates.
[[prepared]]Prevention of old or abandoned transplants remaining in the area.
[[prepared]]Inspection of weeds before removal to determine whether the pest is present.
[[prepared]]Installation of traps in locations suitable for detecting incoming moths.

items.text: Removed plants should not be left in piles near the nursery because larvae may continue feeding or pupate and later produce adult moths.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Removed plants should not be left in piles near the nursery because larvae may continue feeding or pupate and later produce adult moths.

items.text: Nursery Types and Risk Level
Fully Enclosed Nursery
This nursery is equipped with a structure, insect-proof screens, and controllable doors.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This nursery is equipped with a structure, insect-proof screens, and controllable doors.

items.text: It provides a high level of protection when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It provides a high level of protection when:

items: Clean transplants and supplies are used.

items.text: An enclosed nursery is not safe when infestation enters with plants, tools, or contaminated containers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An enclosed nursery is not safe when infestation enters with plants, tools, or contaminated containers.

items.text: Semi-Protected Nursery
This nursery may be partially covered or contain inadequately sealed openings.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This nursery may be partially covered or contain inadequately sealed openings.

items.text: It requires greater emphasis on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It requires greater emphasis on:

items.text: Open Nursery
An open nursery is more exposed to moth immigration, particularly when located near tomato crops.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An open nursery is more exposed to moth immigration, particularly when located near tomato crops.

items.text: Protection depends largely on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Protection depends largely on:

items: Prevention of transplants remaining after the scheduled date.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevention of transplants remaining after the scheduled date.

items.text: Insect-Proof Screens and Barriers
Insect-proof screens help reduce moth entry into protected nurseries.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Insect-proof screens help reduce moth entry into protected nurseries.

items.text: Their efficacy depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Their efficacy depends on:

items: Mesh size being suitable for moth size.
Maintenance of adequate ventilation for transplants.
Prevention of entry of infested plants from outside.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Mesh size being suitable for moth size.
[[prepared]]Prevention of entry of infested plants from outside.

items.text: Screens on walls are insufficient when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Screens on walls are insufficient when:

items: Host weeds occur inside the structure.

items.text: Screens should be inspected periodically, particularly at:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Screens should be inspected periodically, particularly at:

items: Points where screens join the structure.
Locations through which hoses and cables pass.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Locations through which hoses and cables pass.

items.text: Doors and Entry Management
Protected nurseries should preferably use a system that reduces direct insect entry, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Protected nurseries should preferably use a system that reduces direct insect entry, such as:

items: Two doors that are not opened simultaneously.
A vestibule between the outside and inside.
Air or screen curtains where appropriate.
Immediate door closure after workers pass.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Two doors that are not opened simultaneously.
[[prepared]]A vestibule between the outside and inside.

items.text: Entry of the following should be prohibited:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Entry of the following should be prohibited:

items.text: Nursery visits should be scheduled after visiting clean fields and before entering heavily infested fields, not after direct work in infested fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nursery visits should be scheduled after visiting clean fields and before entering heavily infested fields, not after direct work in infested fields.

items.text: Internal Nursery Sanitation
Internal sanitation includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Internal sanitation includes:

items: Avoiding retention of rejected plants among trays.
Disposing of surplus transplants when no longer needed.
Preventing the presence of large or fruiting tomato plants inside the nursery.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoiding retention of rejected plants among trays.
[[prepared]]Disposing of surplus transplants when no longer needed.
[[prepared]]Preventing the presence of large or fruiting tomato plants inside the nursery.

items.text: A small discarded leaf may contain a larva capable of completing development. Waste should therefore be collected in a closed container and not discarded near the nursery.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A small discarded leaf may contain a larva capable of completing development. Waste should therefore be collected in a closed container and not discarded near the nursery.

items.text: Trays and Growing Medium
Tuta absoluta is not usually transmitted with tomato seed itself; transfer risk is associated with infested plants and plant parts.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is not usually transmitted with tomato seed itself; transfer risk is associated with infested plants and plant parts.

items.text: Nevertheless, trays and growing media require attention because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nevertheless, trays and growing media require attention because of:

items: Plant residues adhering to reused trays.
Pupae occurring in cracks or beneath benches.
Movement of trays from an infested nursery into a clean one.
Mixing of old and new batches.
prepared_items: [[prepared]][[term:pupation]]Pupae[[/term]] occurring in cracks or beneath benches.
[[prepared]]Movement of trays from an infested nursery into a clean one.

items.text: Measures include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measures include:

items: Storing trays away from infested tomato crops.
Keeping new trays away from residue piles.
Using growing media free from host-plant residues.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Storing trays away from infested tomato crops.
[[prepared]]Keeping new trays away from residue piles.
[[prepared]]Using growing media free from host-plant residues.

items.text: Separation of Batches Within the Nursery
Each transplant batch should be treated as an independent unit.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each transplant batch should be treated as an independent unit.

items.text: The following should be recorded for each batch:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be recorded for each batch:

items.text: Clear walkways should preferably be maintained between batches to allow:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Clear walkways should preferably be maintained between batches to allow:

items.text: Mixing trays without identification makes it difficult to identify the infestation source after it appears in the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Mixing trays without identification makes it difficult to identify the infestation source after it appears in the field.

items.text: Receiving Batches from Outside the Nursery
When transplants or plants are received from another source, they should not be placed directly among healthy transplants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When transplants or plants are received from another source, they should not be placed directly among healthy transplants.

items.text: The following procedure should be applied:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following procedure should be applied:

items: 1. Place them in a separate receiving area.
3. Review trap records or reports from the supplying nursery.
4. Inspect a representative number of trays.
7. Isolate the batch when suspicion exists.
8. Integrate it only after confirming that it is clean.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Place them in a separate receiving area.
[[prepared]]3. Review trap records or reports from the supplying nursery.
[[prepared]]4. Inspect a representative number of trays.
[[prepared]]7. Isolate the batch when suspicion exists.
[[prepared]]8. Integrate it only after confirming that it is clean.

items.text: A short isolation period helps reveal mines that were not visible at the time of receipt.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A short isolation period helps reveal mines that were not visible at the time of receipt.

items.text: Pheromone Monitoring Inside the Nursery
Pheromone traps are used for early detection of male Tuta absoluta moths.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Pheromone traps are used for early detection of male Tuta absoluta moths.

items.text: They help to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They help to:

items.text: Traps should be placed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps should be placed:

items.text: The number of traps should be determined according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The number of traps should be determined according to:

items.text: A single trap should not be relied upon in large or divided nurseries because it may not represent all areas.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A single trap should not be relied upon in large or divided nurseries because it may not represent all areas.

items.text: The trap remains a tool for monitoring males and cannot by itself confirm that transplants are free from eggs and larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The trap remains a tool for monitoring males and cannot by itself confirm that transplants are free from eggs and larvae.

items.text: Trap Maintenance
Trap maintenance should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Trap maintenance should include:

items: Removing excess insects when they obstruct counting.
Replacing the sticky card when full or dirty.
Replacing the lure according to product instructions.
Avoiding direct hand contact with lures when odor transfer is possible.
Avoiding mixing lures for different species.
Securing the trap in a clearly identified and recorded location.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Removing excess insects when they obstruct counting.
[[prepared]]Replacing the sticky card when full or dirty.
[[prepared]]Replacing the lure according to product instructions.
[[prepared]]Avoiding direct hand contact with lures when odor transfer is possible.
[[prepared]]Securing the trap in a clearly identified and recorded location.

items.text: Catch should be recorded for each trap separately rather than only as a general total because spatial distribution may reveal the moth-entry direction.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Catch should be recorded for each trap separately rather than only as a general total because spatial distribution may reveal the moth-entry direction.

items.text: Plant Inspection Inside the Nursery
Direct inspection of transplants is more important than relying on traps alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Direct inspection of transplants is more important than relying on traps alone.

items.text: Inspection should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should include:

items: Areas where traps have captured moths.

items.text: Inspection frequency should increase when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection frequency should increase when:

items: Temperatures become favorable for pest development.

items.text: Sampling Method
Inspection should not be limited to one row or one nursery area.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should not be limited to one row or one nursery area.

items.text: A route covering the entire nursery should be followed, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A route covering the entire nursery should be followed, such as:

items: Sampling points distributed among corners and the center.
Increased sampling near doors and edges.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Sampling points distributed among corners and the center.

items.text: At each point, transplants should be examined from:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At each point, transplants should be examined from:

items: Upper and lower shelves when trays are placed on racks.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Upper and lower shelves when trays are placed on racks.

items.text: No single universal sample size applies to all nurseries. Sample size should be determined according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single universal sample size applies to all nurseries. Sample size should be determined according to:

items.text: Inspection must be sufficient to detect small infestation foci before they are distributed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection must be sufficient to detect small infestation foci before they are distributed.

items.text: Searching for Eggs
Tuta absoluta eggs are small and difficult to see without training.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta eggs are small and difficult to see without training.

items.text: They should be searched for mainly on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They should be searched for mainly on:

items.text: Eggs initially appear:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Eggs initially appear:

items.text: When necessary, the following can be used:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When necessary, the following can be used:

items: Examination of selected leaves under suitable magnification.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Examination of selected leaves under suitable magnification.

items.text: Absence of visible eggs should not be assumed to mean that the batch is pest-free because larvae may already have hatched and entered leaves.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Absence of visible eggs should not be assumed to mean that the batch is pest-free because larvae may already have hatched and entered leaves.

items.text: Searching for Recent Mines
Mines on transplants begin as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Mines on transplants begin as:

items: A short mine near the leaf base or vein.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A short mine near the leaf base or vein.

items.text: The following should be distinguished:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be distinguished:

items: An active mine containing a larva.

items.text: A suspicious mine should be opened using a fine instrument while searching for:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A suspicious mine should be opened using a fine instrument while searching for:

items: A small larva with a distinct head.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A small larva with a distinct head.

items.text: Inspection of Growing Points
Growing-point infestation in transplants may be more serious than a limited mine in a lower leaf.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Growing-point infestation in transplants may be more serious than a limited mine in a lower leaf.

items.text: Signs include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Signs include:

items: Young leaves held together by silken threads.
A hole in the tender stem.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Young leaves held together by silken threads.

items.text: A transplant that has lost its growing point or is severely deformed should be discarded even if the larva is no longer present because transplant quality and uniformity have declined.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A transplant that has lost its growing point or is severely deformed should be discarded even if the larva is no longer present because transplant quality and uniformity have declined.

items.text: Distinguishing Tuta from Leaf-Miner Flies
Liriomyza leaf-miner flies may occur in nurseries and produce mines that differ from those of Tuta.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]][[term:liriomyza]]Liriomyza[[/term]] leaf-miner flies may occur in nurseries and produce mines that differ from those of Tuta.

items.text: Diagnosis should not be based on mine shape alone, but also on the larva and adult insect.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Diagnosis should not be based on mine shape alone, but also on the larva and adult insect.

items.text: Nursery Alert Levels
Nursery status can be divided into practical alert levels.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nursery status can be divided into practical alert levels.

items.text: Green Level: No Evidence
Preventive monitoring continues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preventive monitoring continues.

items.text: Yellow Level: Suspicion or Limited Catch

items: One moth or a limited number of moths recorded.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]One moth or a limited number of moths recorded.

items.text: Inspection should be intensified and barriers reviewed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection should be intensified and barriers reviewed.

items.text: Orange Level: Limited Confirmed Infestation

items: A larva or active mine in a limited number of trays.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A larva or active mine in a limited number of trays.

items.text: The focus is isolated, its extent is determined, and tray movement is stopped.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The focus is isolated, its extent is determined, and tray movement is stopped.

items.text: Red Level: Widespread Infestation

items: Infestation in more than one batch.
Difficulty defining the boundaries of the focus.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Difficulty defining the boundaries of the focus.

items.text: Suitability of the batches for transfer must be reviewed, and rejection may be safer than introducing them into the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Suitability of the batches for transfer must be reviewed, and rejection may be safer than introducing them into the field.

items.text: These levels are not formal economic thresholds, but an operational system for organizing nursery response.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These levels are not formal economic thresholds, but an operational system for organizing nursery response.

items.text: Response to the First Detection
When the first confirmed egg, larva, or mine is detected:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When the first confirmed egg, larva, or mine is detected:

items: 2. Stop movement of trays from that batch.
8. Determine whether the infestation is individual, localized, or widespread.
9. Record the event with date and location.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]2. Stop movement of trays from that batch.
[[prepared]]8. Determine whether the infestation is individual, localized, or widespread.
[[prepared]]9. Record the event with date and location.

items.text: Infested trays should not be moved to another open location without containment because this may distribute the pest within the nursery.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested trays should not be moved to another open location without containment because this may distribute the pest within the nursery.

items.text: Removal of Infested Transplants
When removing infested transplants:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When removing infested transplants:

items: Pull out the complete plant when necessary.
Place it directly into a sealed bag or container.
Do not leave it in walkways.
Do not discard it on an open compost pile.
Do not use it for gap filling in fields.
Dispose of it using a method that kills the pest stages present.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Pull out the complete plant when necessary.
[[prepared]]Place it directly into a sealed bag or container.
[[prepared]]Do not discard it on an open compost pile.
[[prepared]]Do not use it for gap filling in fields.
[[prepared]]Dispose of it using a method that kills the pest stages present.

items.text: Suitable disposal methods, subject to local regulations and available resources, include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Suitable disposal methods, subject to local regulations and available resources, include:

items: Secure sealing followed by safe disposal.
Fine shredding combined with suitable treatment.
Heat exposure sufficient to kill all stages.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Heat exposure sufficient to kill all stages.

items.text: Burning residues should not be treated as an automatic option unless it is legally permitted and environmentally safe.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Burning residues should not be treated as an automatic option unless it is legally permitted and environmentally safe.

items.text: When Should an Individual Transplant Be Rejected?
A transplant should be discarded when it carries:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A transplant should be discarded when it carries:

items.text: A transplant bearing an old empty mine may also be discarded if the damage has weakened it or if absence of other pest stages cannot be confirmed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A transplant bearing an old empty mine may also be discarded if the damage has weakened it or if absence of other pest stages cannot be confirmed.

items.text: When Should Surrounding Trays Be Isolated?
Surrounding trays should be isolated when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Surrounding trays should be isolated when:

items: Their leaves contact those of infested trays.
Several infested transplants occur at the same site.
Trays were moved before the focus was discovered.
Advanced larvae capable of movement are present.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Their leaves contact those of infested trays.
[[prepared]]Several infested transplants occur at the same site.
[[prepared]]Trays were moved before the focus was discovered.
[[prepared]]Advanced larvae capable of movement are present.

items.text: Removing only the visibly infested transplant without examining surrounding plants is insufficient because eggs and young larvae may be difficult to detect.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Removing only the visibly infested transplant without examining surrounding plants is insufficient because eggs and young larvae may be difficult to detect.

items.text: Decision to Accept or Reject a Batch
The decision depends on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The decision depends on:

items: Infestation occurring in more than one tray.
Risk of introducing the pest into the field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Infestation occurring in more than one tray.
[[prepared]]Risk of introducing the pest into the field.

items.text: A Batch May Be Accepted After Verification When:

items: No new infestation appeared during reinspection.
Trap catches were stable or absent.
Remaining plants could be confirmed as clean.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Remaining plants could be confirmed as clean.

items.text: A Batch Should Tend Toward Rejection When:

items: Larvae of different instars are present.
Transplants from several batches are mixed.
Freedom of remaining plants from infestation cannot be guaranteed.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Freedom of remaining plants from infestation cannot be guaranteed.

items.text: The decision should be more conservative when the transplants are destined for a low-infestation area or a large project.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The decision should be more conservative when the transplants are destined for a low-infestation area or a large project.

items.text: Role of Biological Control in the Nursery
Biological control may be integrated into nursery management, particularly in facilities that retain transplants for long periods.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological control may be integrated into nursery management, particularly in facilities that retain transplants for long periods.

items.text: General options include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]General options include:

items: Use of egg parasitoids where appropriate.
Use of microbial products targeting young larvae.
Avoidance of insecticides highly harmful to natural enemies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Use of microbial products targeting young larvae.
[[prepared]]Avoidance of insecticides highly harmful to natural enemies.

items.text: Biological control should not be used to justify transfer of transplants carrying confirmed infestation into the field. The principal nursery objective is to produce plants that are as free from infestation as possible.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological control should not be used to justify transfer of transplants carrying confirmed infestation into the field. The principal nursery objective is to produce plants that are as free from infestation as possible.

items.text: Release programs require:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Release programs require:

items.text: These aspects will be explained in detail in the biological-control sections.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These aspects will be explained in detail in the biological-control sections.

items.text: Use of Insecticides in the Nursery
The nursery program should not be based on repeated calendar spraying.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery program should not be based on repeated calendar spraying.

items.text: When intervention is required, the following should be considered:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When intervention is required, the following should be considered:

items: Determining whether eggs or larvae are present.
Achieving good coverage of lower leaf surfaces.
Considering the effect on natural enemies.
Not considering spraying automatic proof that transplants are pest-free.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Determining whether eggs or larvae are present.
[[prepared]]Achieving good coverage of lower leaf surfaces.
[[prepared]]Not considering spraying automatic proof that transplants are pest-free.

items.text: Larvae inside mines or growing points may survive. Post-treatment inspection therefore remains necessary.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Larvae inside mines or growing points may survive. Post-treatment inspection therefore remains necessary.

items.text: Risks of Intensive Spraying in the Nursery
Excessive spraying may cause:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Excessive spraying may cause:

items: Concealment of symptoms without elimination of infestation.
Transfer of apparently healthy transplants that still carry live larvae.
Beginning the field season with a long history of exposure to chemical groups.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Concealment of symptoms without elimination of infestation.
[[prepared]]Transfer of apparently healthy transplants that still carry live larvae.
[[prepared]]Beginning the field season with a long history of exposure to chemical groups.

items.text: Every product used in the nursery should be recorded so that the history of modes of action can be considered when developing the field program.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Every product used in the nursery should be recorded so that the history of modes of action can be considered when developing the field program.

items.text: Transplant Treatment Record
The record for each batch should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The record for each batch should include:

rows: Eggs, mines, larvae, and growing points
IRAC group number when an insecticide is used
prepared_rows: [[prepared]][[term:irac]]IRAC group[[/term]] number when an insecticide is used

items.text: Treatment history should be delivered to the field manager so that the same chemical group is not repeated unnecessarily.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatment history should be delivered to the field manager so that the same chemical group is not repeated unnecessarily.

items.text: Transplant Hardening Stage
Before transplanting, plants may be moved into a hardening area that is more open than the nursery.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before transplanting, plants may be moved into a hardening area that is more open than the nursery.

items.text: This stage is a critical point because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This stage is a critical point because:

items: Their retention period may become longer.
Batches prepared for shipment are placed close together.
Entry of workers and transport vehicles increases.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Batches prepared for shipment are placed close together.
[[prepared]]Entry of workers and transport vehicles increases.

items.text: Hardening-stage measures include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Hardening-stage measures include:

items: Selecting an area away from infested fields.
Avoiding leaving transplants unprotected overnight where possible.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Selecting an area away from infested fields.
[[prepared]]Avoiding leaving transplants unprotected overnight where possible.

items.text: Final Inspection Before Dispatch
Final inspection should be conducted immediately before shipment and should include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Final inspection should be conducted immediately before shipment and should include:

items: Inspecting a representative sample from all parts of the batch.
Confirming that no infested leaves occur beneath trays.
Confirming cleanliness of the transport vehicle.
Attaching an identification label to the batch.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Inspecting a representative sample from all parts of the batch.
[[prepared]]Confirming that no infested leaves occur beneath trays.
[[prepared]]Attaching an identification label to the batch.

items.text: Health certification should not depend on the most recent treatment alone, but on inspection and traceability results.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Health certification should not depend on the most recent treatment alone, but on inspection and traceability results.

items.text: Transport of Transplants
During transport:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During transport:

items: Transplants should not be transported with infested tomato fruits.
Contaminated harvest crates should not be used.
Leaf and plant residues should not be allowed to accumulate between trays.
Stops near infested fields should be minimized.
The load should be covered in a manner that reduces moth exposure without suffocating transplants.
Identification of each batch should be maintained.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Transplants should not be transported with infested tomato fruits.
[[prepared]]Contaminated harvest crates should not be used.
[[prepared]]Leaf and plant residues should not be allowed to accumulate between trays.
[[prepared]]Stops near infested fields should be minimized.
[[prepared]]The load should be covered in a manner that reduces moth exposure without suffocating transplants.
[[prepared]]Identification of each batch should be maintained.

items.text: Vehicles previously used to carry infested plants or fruits should be cleaned.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Vehicles previously used to carry infested plants or fruits should be cleaned.

items.text: Receiving Transplants in the Field
Transplants should be inspected when they arrive at the field even if they were inspected at the nursery.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Transplants should be inspected when they arrive at the field even if they were inspected at the nursery.

items.text: Infestation may occur during:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation may occur during:

items.text: Receiving procedures include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Receiving procedures include:

items: 7. Record where each batch is planted.
8. Retain records of source and treatments.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]7. Record where each batch is planted.
[[prepared]]8. Retain records of source and treatments.

items.text: Distribution of Batches Within the Field
Transplants from different sources should preferably not be mixed randomly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Transplants from different sources should preferably not be mixed randomly.

items.text: The location of each batch should be recorded because this helps interpret the pattern of infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The location of each batch should be recorded because this helps interpret the pattern of infestation.

items.text: If infestation appears:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]If infestation appears:

items: Distributed within one sector, transplants may be the source.
Concentrated along one edge, external immigration may be the source.
Associated with one cultivar or source, source-related or susceptibility differences may be involved.
Near the tray-storage area, infestation may have occurred during waiting.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Distributed within one sector, transplants may be the source.
[[prepared]]Concentrated along one edge, external immigration may be the source.
[[prepared]]Associated with one cultivar or source, source-related or susceptibility differences may be involved.
[[prepared]]Near the tray-storage area, infestation may have occurred during waiting.

items.text: Batch maps facilitate rapid decisions on intensive inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Batch maps facilitate rapid decisions on intensive inspection.

items.text: Management of Surplus Transplants
Surplus transplants become a risk when left:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Surplus transplants become a risk when left:

items: Until they grow into permanent hosts.

items.text: The following should be implemented:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should be implemented:

items: Determine the number required for gap filling.
Retain them for a defined period.
Dispose of them after the gap-filling period ends.
Do not allow them to become large infested tomato plants.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Determine the number required for gap filling.
[[prepared]]Dispose of them after the gap-filling period ends.
[[prepared]]Do not allow them to become large infested tomato plants.

items.text: Surplus transplants may become a source of moths even when the field itself is under a good management program.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Surplus transplants may become a source of moths even when the field itself is under a good management program.

items.text: Gap Filling
When transplants are used to replace missing plants:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When transplants are used to replace missing plants:

items: Do not use transplants from a suspicious batch.
Record the date and source of gap filling.
Do not move transplants from an infested field into another field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Do not use transplants from a suspicious batch.
[[prepared]]Record the date and source of gap filling.
[[prepared]]Do not move transplants from an infested field into another field.

items.text: Late gap filling with unmonitored transplants introduces a new infestation source into the crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Late gap filling with unmonitored transplants introduces a new infestation source into the crop.

items.text: Indicators of Successful Nursery Management
Indicators include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Indicators include:

items: No confirmed infestation inside the nursery.
Absence of distributed early infestation in the field.
Ability to trace any focus to its source.
Reduced need for rescue sprays after transplanting.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Absence of distributed early infestation in the field.
[[prepared]]Ability to trace any focus to its source.
[[prepared]]Reduced need for rescue sprays after transplanting.

items.text: Absence of trap catches alone does not indicate program success unless it agrees with plant-inspection results.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Absence of trap catches alone does not indicate program success unless it agrees with plant-inspection results.

items.text: Indicators of Nursery-Management Failure
These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items: Appearance of mines during the first days after transplanting in scattered sectors.
Infestation of several plants from the same source.
Detection of advanced larvae immediately after transplanting.
Dead growing points before plants become established.
Mixing batches and losing source identification.
Presence of infested surplus transplants near the field.
Continued appearance of infestation after removal of a limited number of plants.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Appearance of mines during the first days after transplanting in scattered sectors.
[[prepared]]Infestation of several plants from the same source.
[[prepared]]Detection of advanced larvae immediately after transplanting.
[[prepared]]Dead growing points before plants become established.
[[prepared]]Presence of infested surplus transplants near the field.
[[prepared]]Continued appearance of infestation after removal of a limited number of plants.

items.text: Nursery Checklist
Sample Transplant-Batch Inspection Record
The record should also include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The record should also include:

items.text: Special Considerations for Egyptian Nurseries
Under Egyptian conditions, risk of Tuta movement from nurseries increases because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Under Egyptian conditions, risk of Tuta movement from nurseries increases because of:

items: Overlapping tomato planting seasons throughout the year.
Presence of open nurseries near fields.
Presence of old fields in which control has stopped.
Retention of surplus transplants near farmland.
High temperatures that accelerate egg hatching and larval development.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Overlapping tomato planting seasons throughout the year.
[[prepared]]Presence of old fields in which control has stopped.
[[prepared]]High temperatures that accelerate egg hatching and larval development.

items.text: Practical priorities are therefore:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Practical priorities are therefore:

items: 1. Do not establish a nursery beside an old field.
3. Inspect growing points and leaves at least twice weekly, increasing frequency when risk rises.
5. Do not transport batches carrying confirmed infestation.
7. Record insecticides used in the nursery.
9. Remove surplus transplants after gap filling is completed.
10. Monitor the first three weeks after transplanting as a period for detecting introduced infestation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Do not establish a nursery beside an old field.
[[prepared]]3. Inspect growing points and leaves at least twice weekly, increasing frequency when risk rises.
[[prepared]]5. Do not transport batches carrying confirmed infestation.
[[prepared]]7. Record insecticides used in the nursery.
[[prepared]]9. Remove surplus transplants after gap filling is completed.
[[prepared]]10. Monitor the first three weeks after transplanting as a period for detecting introduced infestation.

items.text: The number of inspections is not a fixed rule. A high-risk nursery may require more frequent inspection.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The number of inspections is not a fixed rule. A high-risk nursery may require more frequent inspection.

items.text: Common Errors in Nursery Management
Error One: Assuming the Nursery Is Free from Tuta
The moth can lay eggs on small transplants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The moth can lay eggs on small transplants.

items.text: Error Two: Relying on Traps Alone
Traps monitor males and do not detect all eggs and larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Traps monitor males and do not detect all eggs and larvae.

items.text: Error Three: Treating Spraying as Proof of Pest-Free Transplants
Larvae may remain inside mines or growing points.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Larvae may remain inside mines or growing points.

items.text: Error Four: Removing an Infested Transplant and Leaving It Beside the Nursery
The larva may continue developing and later produce a moth.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The larva may continue developing and later produce a moth.

items.text: Error Five: Mixing Batches
This prevents source tracing and makes isolation difficult.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This prevents source tracing and makes isolation difficult.

items.text: Error Six: Neglecting the Hardening Area
Infestation may occur after the protected-production stage ends.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation may occur after the protected-production stage ends.

items.text: Error Seven: Failing to Inspect Transplants on Arrival
Infestation may enter during transport and waiting.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation may enter during transport and waiting.

items.text: Error Eight: Retaining Surplus Transplants for Too Long
They become a pest reservoir.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They become a pest reservoir.

items.text: Error Nine: Transporting Transplants with Produce Crates
Pest stages may move with residues and containers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Pest stages may move with residues and containers.

items.text: Error Ten: Randomly Using Reduced Rates
Treatment may fail and increase resistance selection pressure.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Treatment may fail and increase resistance selection pressure.

items.text: Error Eleven: Repeating the Same Chemical Group in the Nursery and Field
Selection pressure begins before the field season starts.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Selection pressure begins before the field season starts.

items.text: Error Twelve: Ignoring Growing-Point Infestation
An infested growing point may be more serious than a limited mine in an old leaf.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An infested growing point may be more serious than a limited mine in an old leaf.

items.text: Nursery Management Timeline
Before Transplant Production Begins
After Germination
During Transplant Growth
During Hardening
Before Dispatch
Upon Arrival at the Field

items: Record the location of each batch.
Dispose of trays and residues properly.

items.text: After Transplanting

items: Inspect sectors according to transplant source.
Dispose of surplus transplants after gap filling ends.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Dispose of surplus transplants after gap filling ends.

items.text: Summary of Section Two
The nursery is a critical point in the prevention of Tuta absoluta because transfer of infested transplants establishes the pest in the field from the beginning of the season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery is a critical point in the prevention of Tuta absoluta because transfer of infested transplants establishes the pest in the field from the beginning of the season.

items.text: Nursery management depends on an integrated set of procedures including:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Nursery management depends on an integrated set of procedures including:

items: Selecting a site away from infestation sources.
Direct inspection for eggs, mines, and growing-point infestation.
Reinspection upon arrival at the field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Selecting a site away from infestation sources.
[[prepared]]Direct inspection for eggs, mines, and growing-point infestation.

items.text: Spraying transplants before transport is insufficient to declare them pest-free. The decision must depend on inspection, traceability, and absence of active infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spraying transplants before transport is insufficient to declare them pest-free. The decision must depend on inspection, traceability, and absence of active infestation.

items.text: Each batch should be treated as an independent unit so that its source, inspection results, treatments, and planting location can be identified.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each batch should be treated as an independent unit so that its source, inspection results, treatments, and planting location can be identified.

items.text: The best protection is achieved when nursery management begins before transplant emergence and continues through the first weeks after transplanting in the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The best protection is achieved when nursery management begins before transplant emergence and continues through the first weeks after transplanting in the field.
